name: Profile and Audit React Performance
description: Use Lighthouse, React DevTools Profiler, and Chrome Performance tab to identify and fix rendering bottlenecks.
trendingReason: Core Web Vitals directly impact SEO ranking, making performance audits critical

steps.title: Run a Lighthouse audit from the CLI
steps.description: Generate a full Lighthouse report including performance, accessibility, and SEO scores.
steps.tip: Run against a production build (pnpm build && pnpm start) for accurate scores.

steps.title: Analyze Core Web Vitals
steps.description: Check Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, and Cumulative Layout Shift.

steps.title: Profile React renders with why-did-you-render
steps.description: Install why-did-you-render to log unnecessary re-renders to the console.
steps.tip: Only enable in development. Import it before React in your entry file.

steps.title: Measure component render time with React Profiler
steps.description: Wrap components in React.Profiler to log render durations programmatically.
steps.command: echo '<Profiler id="MyComponent" onRender={(id, phase, duration) => console.log(id, phase, duration)}>...</ Profiler>'

steps.title: Run multiple Lighthouse audits for stable results
steps.description: Use lighthouse-ci to run multiple audits and get median scores.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# React Performance Audit
set -euo pipefail

URL=${1:-http://localhost:3000}

echo "=== Running Lighthouse Performance Audit ==="
npx lighthouse "$URL" \
  --output=html \
  --output-path=./lighthouse-report.html \
  --chrome-flags='--headless=new' \
  --only-categories=performance,accessibility

echo ""
echo "=== Report saved to lighthouse-report.html ==="

echo ""
echo "=== Quick Performance Tips ==="
echo "1. Use React.lazy() for code splitting"
echo "2. Memoize expensive computations with useMemo"
echo "3. Virtualize long lists with @tanstack/react-virtual"
echo "4. Optimize images with next/image or sharp"
echo "5. Preload critical assets with <link rel='preload'>"

faq.question: Why is my Lighthouse score different every time?
faq.answer: Lighthouse scores vary due to CPU load, network conditions, and browser state. Always test against a production build, close other tabs, and run 3-5 times to get a median score.

faq.question: How do I reduce unnecessary re-renders in React?
faq.answer: Use React.memo for pure components, useMemo/useCallback for expensive values and stable references, and avoid creating new objects or arrays in render. The React DevTools Profiler highlights which components re-render and why.

faq.question: What is a good Lighthouse performance score?
faq.answer: Aim for 90+ on desktop and 70+ on mobile. Scores above 90 are considered good, 50-89 need improvement, and below 50 are poor.
